title: Circles and arcs from the midpoints of four sides (full overlapping circles)

rule.instruction: Circles and arcs from the midpoints of four sides. (ACG 59)

restrictions: USER-PICKED, revisiting #138 (first done as round 14 with the asymmetric-scatter take). Chosen NEW angle: FULL OVERLAPPING CIRCLES — whole rings (not just clipped arcs) blooming from the four side midpoints, overlapping into a moire/interference field. Uses `arcs` with a new `inset` lever pulling the midpoint centres inward so circles close inside the frame. Executed organic: hand-drawn (jitter) always, most designs add mild irregularity (radiusJitter / small centreJitter) so the moire reads hand-made not CAD. Monochrome; density capped so overlapping rings stay open, never a solid ink patch (paper-rip limit). The 16 span inset depth (how central/complete the rings), ring count & size (overlap amount), 4 vs 8 ring-families (+corners), and hand energy / irregularity.
